notes.note: Rufus is L. Mescinius Rufus, who had served as Cicero's quaestor in Cilicia (51-50 BC); the quaestor-imperator tie was among the most respected bonds in Roman public life. Cicero contrasts that official service with the freer goodwill Rufus now offers.

notes.anchor: the partnership of the danger or the labour
notes.note: Societatem vel periculi vel laboris: written in late April 49 BC, as Cicero agonised over whether to leave Italy and join Pompey against Caesar. He hesitates to draw Rufus into so dangerous a commitment by openly sharing his plan.

notes.anchor: What is right is plain; what is expedient is dark
notes.note: Quid rectum sit apparet, quid expediat obscurum est: a compressed statement of the central problem of Cicero's later De Officiis, the supposed clash between the honourable (honestum) and the expedient (utile), here applied to his choice in the civil war.
